programme.title: Half Man
programme.desc: 1987. Ruben and Niall form an intoxicating bond in this intimate and epic saga. Contains very strong language, some violence and upsetting scenes.

programme.title: Forensics: The Real CSI
programme.desc: Police race to identify an intruder after a child is sexually assaulted in her bedroom. Contains scenes which some viewers may find disturbing.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: It's a sibling showdown in West Yorkshire with Danny Sebastian.

programme.title: Secret Garden
programme.desc: David Attenborough reveals how tawny owls, badgers, dormice and bumblebees have made a home in a beautiful woodland garden hidden at the bottom of a steep-sided Welsh valley.

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Kelly Cates presents highlights of three Premier League matches. Manchester United host Liverpool, Aston Villa are at home against Tottenham Hotspur and Crystal Palace travel to Bournemouth.

programme.title: The Titfield Thunderbolt
programme.desc: Ealing comedy about a group of villagers who battle to preserve the local railway line by running it themselves, until they are sabotaged in a midnight raid by a rival bus company.

programme.title: Whisky Galore!
programme.desc: Classic Ealing comedy about the mayhem that ensues when a whisky-laden ship runs aground off the coast of the Outer Hebrides during the Second World War.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of day 17 of the World Snooker Championship at the Crucible Theatre, Sheffield.

programme.title: Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott introduces classic recipes from some of the BBC's best-loved television chefs. These dishes will help when you are planning the perfect picnic.

programme.title: Vintage Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Curios and artefacts brought along by the public in Witney, Oxfordshire, include seascapes brought over from Holland and an album of watercolours.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin and his team of experts invite members of the public to bring their antiques to be viewed and valued, with an option to sell at auction.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Jordan Banjo, Sian Lloyd, Nick Pickard and Ellie White test their general knowledge skills on this week's House of Games.

programme.title: Great Korean Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael begins his journey at the tense frontier of the Demilitarised Zone.

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of day 17 of the World Snooker Championship at the Crucible Theatre, Sheffield.

programme.title: The Great Escaper
programme.desc: WWII veteran Bernard sneaks out of his care home to attend the 70th anniversary of D-Day. Contains some strong language.

programme.title: Mud
programme.desc: Two young lads join a spirited mystery man's quest to reunite with his one true love, unaware of the forces and feelings that must be faced.

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Adam Henson and Sammi Kinghorn head to the Vale of Evesham, where fertile soils and a mild microclimate have long made it one of Britain's most productive growing regions.

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: The teams discover their next checkpoint lies in Zaamin National Park, Uzbekistan.

programme.title: Inside Barlinnie
programme.desc: Billy's new start is on a knife edge, and Alan is tempted back to his old ways. Contains some strong language.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: Kirsty Wilson and Brian Cunningham start growing a wide variety of edible produce, and Brian provides an update on the veg he sowed at the start of the series.

programme.title: Great Japanese Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael explores the history of Hokkaido's indigenous Ainu people.

programme.title: Farm 999
programme.desc: Fly-tippers are caught by angry farmers in Warwickshire. Grazing land and livestock in the Mourne mountains face raging fires, and Paul Colgan meets a domestic abuse survivor.

programme.title: Lost and Found in the Lakes
programme.desc: The team rake over a jetty, desperately seeking a treasured lost ring, and social media detective Mui gets creative, hoping to trace the owner of a phone found in the depths.

programme.title: See Hear: Love and Dating
programme.desc: A look at love and relationships in the deaf community, from a couple married for over 50 years to honest conversations about modern dating and finding connection today.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The big political issues of the day.

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Game show in which players score points by answering questions correctly while avoiding the impossible answers.

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Game show in which players score points by answering questions correctly while avoiding the impossible answers.

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts the quickfire quiz where six plucky contestants must stay out of the light to be all right. Can anyone beat the lightning and win the £3,000 jackpot.

programme.title: Clive Myrie's Caribbean Adventure
programme.desc: Clive gets his steps in as he learns to salsa dance with the locals, discovers the secrets that have kept this historic old city standing, and explores the dark history of sugar.

programme.title: Big Cats 24/7
programme.desc: The team begins a six-month expedition following lions, leopards and cheetahs in the Okavango Delta. A huge wildfire threatens both the big cats and the team.

programme.title: Vintage Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Michael Aspel and a team of experts invite members of the public to bring along their antiques for examination. This edition comes from Mellerstain House.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Today Flog It is at the RAF Museum at Hendon in London. There's a photo album of RAF Hendon in the 30s and a rare picture that brings a surprise in the saleroom.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Jordan Banjo, Sian Lloyd, Nick Pickard and Ellie White test their general knowledge skills on this week's House of Games.

programme.title: Great Korean Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael's South Korean railway journey continues in Seoul. Travelling across one of the world's largest metro systems, he heads south to Suwon to visit Samsung's headquarters.

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotland's Rivers
programme.desc: Paul explores the Upper Dee from the Cairngorm Mountains to Balmoral Castle and Royal Deeside, and learns about a lost German Zeppelin and Queen Victoria's 'darling' man.

programme.title: Planet Earth II: A World of Wonder
programme.desc: A compilation from the wildlife documentary series presented by David Attenborough, visiting animals in their habitats across the globe, recorded in stunning ultra-high definition.

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig takes a QI look at vices and virtues with Eshaan Akbar, Jo Brand, Rhys James and Alan Davies. Contains some strong language.

programme.title: This Is a Bomb: The Nevada Casino Heist
programme.desc: Unbeknownst to the FBI, a quiet Hungarian immigrant landscaper, with no apparent ties to organised crime and no known record, concocts an elaborate extortion plan.

programme.title: Newsnight, followed by Weather
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the crucial news stories of the day. Followed by Weather.

programme.title: The People's Piazza: A History of Covent Garden
programme.desc: David Olusoga explores the 400-year history of London's Covent Garden Piazza.

programme.title: Waves
programme.desc: Tensions beneath the surface cause Florida teen Tyler to sink self-destructively, devastating those closest to him, including his sister Emily. Contains very strong language.

programme.title: See Hear: Love and Dating
programme.desc: A look at love and relationships in the deaf community, from a couple married for over 50 years to honest conversations about modern dating and finding connection today.

programme.title: Twenty Twenty Six
programme.desc: Sarah's gone viral, and rumours about a famous footballer put everyone under pressure. Contains some strong language.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.
